summary: deployment-key-auditor exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package's package.json declares a lifecycle script that requires child_process, collects hostname/env/identity data, and POSTs it to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js compounds this with a full host-enumeration payload: it requires os/fs/https/child_process, calls os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync, and ships the collected data via https.request/https.get POST and curl invocations to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (referenced multiple times, including lines 161 and 323). The destination is an anonymous github.io subdomain unrelated to any legitimate 'deployment key auditor' purpose, with no version pinning, no integrity check, and no documented publisher relationship. Installing or running this package causes installer host identifiers, environment variables, and filesystem reconnaissance results to be sent to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
